content: This theme develops computational approaches to understand cognitive and neural mechanisms from high-dimensional data.
title: Computational Neuroscience

title: Contact

content: This placeholder profile shows where future team members will appear once positions are filled.
summary: This profile slot can be replaced as the team grows.
title: Future Lab Member

content:
We are building a research program focused on how neural systems support healthy and disordered cognition, and how those insights can be translated into better diagnostics and interventions.
Our lab combines computational methods, human neuroscience, and clinically grounded questions.
Explore # Research: Current themes and projects. Publications: Papers, preprints, and invited work. People: Current team members and future alumni archive. Open Positions: Opportunities to join the lab. Contact: How to get in touch.
title: Lange Lab

content:
This project integrates behavioral phenotyping and neural signals to derive interpretable biomarkers for longitudinal cognitive outcomes.
Current goals # Build robust multimodal prediction pipelines. Quantify uncertainty and model transportability. Identify markers that are actionable in real-world settings.
summary: Integrating behavioral and neural features to model longitudinal cognitive change.
title: Multimodal Biomarkers for Cognitive Trajectories

title: People

content:
This project builds a framework for mapping computationally defined phenotypes to testable, individualized intervention strategies.
Current goals # Define stratification criteria across heterogeneous cohorts. Benchmark intervention-response prediction methods. Develop reproducible analysis workflows for clinical collaboration.
summary: Linking computational phenotypes to individualized treatment hypotheses.
title: Personalized Intervention Mapping

content:
Publications are maintained as individual markdown entries for simple editing and long-term portability.
If you are looking for preprints or supplemental materials, open a publication page and follow the links.
title: Publications

content: Our work is organized by themes. Each theme includes one or more active projects.
title: Research

content:
Sven M. Lange leads the lab’s research program at the intersection of computational neuroscience and translational mental health science.
Current interests include predictive modeling of cognitive trajectories, precision intervention mapping, and open, reproducible workflows for interdisciplinary neuroscience.
title: Sven M. Lange

content: This theme focuses on converting basic neuroscience findings into clinically actionable tools and interventions.
title: Translational Neuroscience

content:

Links # DOI PDF
summary: A framework for interpretable phenotyping across behavioral and neural modalities.
title: Robust Phenotyping of Cognitive Variability Using Multimodal Neural Data
